name: James K. Polk
role: US President (1845–1849) — Architect of the War
quote: Mexico has passed the boundary of the United States, has invaded our territory and shed American blood upon the American soil.

name: Zachary Taylor
role: US General — Northern Theater; later 12th US President
quote: I have always done my duty. I am ready to die. My only regret is for the friends I leave behind me.

name: Winfield Scott
role: US General-in-Chief — Veracruz to Mexico City Campaign
quote: I have the honor to report that the flag of the United States floats over the National Palace of Mexico.

name: Nicholas P. Trist
role: US Peace Commissioner — Negotiated Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo
quote: If it were possible for me to have done what I considered right without disobeying orders, I would willingly have done it.

name: Stephen W. Kearny
role: US Brigadier General — Army of the West (New Mexico & California)
quote: The United States has proclaimed itself sovereign over this country. It is my duty to maintain that sovereignty.

name: Antonio López de Santa Anna
role: Mexican President-General — Supreme Commander of Mexican Forces
quote: I am the Napoleon of the West. If my fellow citizens sacrifice me in the hour of darkness, I will justify history's ultimate verdict.

name: Mariano Arista
role: Mexican General — Army of the North; defeated at Palo Alto and Resaca de la Palma
quote: The enemy's artillery was devastating. Our troops fought with courage but could not overcome the weight of their fire.

name: Pedro de Ampudia
role: Mexican General — Defender of Monterrey; commanded Army of the North after Arista
quote: I surrender Monterrey under terms that preserve the dignity of the Mexican army and the honor of its soldiers.

name: Manuel de la Peña y Peña
role: Mexican President (1847–1848) — Signed Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo
quote: A dishonorable peace is infinitely preferable to a dishonorable war. We save what remains of the nation.

name: Sam Houston
role: Commander, Texan Army; First President, Republic of Texas; later US Senator
quote: Texas could have avoided this war if she had been willing to surrender her honor. But Texans do not surrender their honor.

name: William Barret Travis
role: Lt. Col., Texas Army — Commander of the Alamo Garrison
quote: I shall never surrender or retreat. Victory or Death. W. Barret Travis, Lt. Col. comdt.

name: Stephen F. Austin
role: 'Father of Texas' — Founder of the Anglo-American Colonies; Texas Secretary of State
quote: The cause of Philanthropy, of humanity, of liberty and of human happiness throughout the world, demands that Texas shall be filled with a civilized and industrious people.

name: Henry Clay
role: US Senator (KY), Whig Leader — Opponent of the War and Texas Annexation
quote: This is no war of defense, but one of unnecessary and of offensive aggression. It is Mexico that is defending her firesides, her castles and her altars, not we.

name: Abraham Lincoln
role: US Representative (IL, 1847–49) — Critic of Polk's War Pretext
quote: Let him [Polk] answer, fully, fairly, and candidly. Let him answer with facts, and not with arguments. Let him remember he sits where Washington sat, and so remembering, let him answer, as Washington would answer.

name: John C. Frémont
role: US Army Captain — Bear Flag Republic; US Military Governor of California
quote: The Bear Flag Republic is the first step in the destiny of the Pacific Coast to become American.

name: José de Urrea
role: Mexican General — Coastal Sweep (1836); urged clemency after Goliad surrender
quote: I begged for the lives of Fannin's men. I gave my word of honor. Santa Anna overruled me — a decision I will never cease to regret.

name: Ulysses S. Grant
role: US Lieutenant (Mexican War); later 18th President — War critic in retrospect
quote: The Mexican War was one of the most unjust ever waged by a stronger against a weaker nation. The Southern Rebellion was largely the outgrowth of the Mexican War.
